summary: The Illinois Quantum and Microelectronics Park launched the On-Ramp program to provide immediate ecosystem access for future tenants through partnerships with mHUB, the UChicago Science Incubator at Hyde Park Labs, and the Discovery Partners Institute. Companies such as IBM, Pasqal, Diraq, and Quantum Machines can now utilize specialized lab spaces and quantum equipment—including cryostats, control electronics, lasers, and a 124-mile fiber optic quantum loop—while permanent facilities are developed. According to Governor JB Pritzker, this initiative removes barriers and accelerates opportunity in Illinois’ quantum sector. Renovations at mHUB, supported by the Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity, are set to complete in Spring 2026, further reinforcing the state’s role in advancing quantum research and industry collaborations.
